item.title: Have You Seen the New Excel?
item.description:
Stop coding. Stop hiring. Stop building.
While the tech world obsesses over large language models and neural networks, I discovered the real disruptor that has been hiding in plain sight. Mine was o

item.title: Copy Fail — 732 Bytes to Root
item.description: CVE-2026-31431. 100% Reliable Linux LPE — no race, no per-distro offsets, page-cache write that bypasses on-disk file-integrity tools and crosses containers. Found by Xint Code.

item.title: Cursor Camp
item.description: A campground for your cursor

item.title: Remote agents in Vibe. Powered by Mistral Medium 3.5. | Mistral AI
item.description: Introducing Mistral Medium 3.5, remote coding agents in Vibe, plus new Work mode in Le Chat for complex tasks.

item.title: Raspberry Pi Connect may control Windows soon
item.description: Support for remote controlling Windows PCs may be added to Raspberry Pi Connect, Raspberry Pi's free remote access service. When they announced Pi Connect in 2024, I speculated the service was launched in response to RealVNC's sluggish adoption of Wayland, leading to Pi users lacking a solid remote access solution after Pi OS 12 'Bookworm' was launched. The service was helpful for those who had one or more Raspberry Pis to access, but the Pi Connect daemon didn't run on Windows or macOS at the time, so a true competitor to RealVNC (at least for basic use cases) it was not.

item.title: Simple Machines
we need a federation of forges
item.description: git is decentralized, but what of the rest?

item.title: Zed is 1.0 - Zed Blog
item.description: From the Zed Blog: The editor we set out to build is now 1.0.
item.author: (Nathan Sobo)

item.title: Soft launch of open-source code platform for government - Digital Government
item.description: Code.overheid.nl platform is now live, enabling government organisations to jointly develop and publish open-source software independently.

item.title: An Interview with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and AWS CEO Matt Garman About Bedrock Managed Agents
item.description: An interview with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and AWS CEO Matt Garman about their new partnership, plus my thoughts on OpenAI and Microsoft’s new deal.

item.title: Bugs Rust Won't Catch | corrode Rust Consulting
item.description: In April 2026, Canonical disclosed 44 CVEs in uutils, the Rust reimplementation of GNU coreutil…

item.title: How ChatGPT serves ads. Here's the full attribution loop.
item.description: OpenAI's ad platform has two halves. On the ChatGPT side, the backend injects structured single_advertiser_ad_unit objects into the conversation SSE stream while the model is responding. On the merchant side, a tracking SDK called OAIQ runs in the visitor's browser and reports product views back to OpenAI.

item.title: GitHub RCE Vulnerability: CVE-2026-3854 Breakdown | Wiz Blog
item.description: A CVSS 8.7 vulnerability in GitHub Enterprise Server allows remote code execution. Read the threat brief and find vulnerable GHES instances from Wiz.
item.author: (https://www.wiz.io/authors/sagi)

item.title: Before GitHub
item.description: Open Source before GitHub was reputation-driven and full of friction
item.author: (Armin Ronacher)

item.title: 10Gb Ethernet: what I had to (re)learn
item.description: Wired networking in the home and small offices has been pretty stagnant for ages, so upgrading my home from 2.5Gb ethernet to 10Gb meant I had to (re)learn a few things.
item.author: (Giles Thomas)

item.title: Anatomy of an Article

item.title: Bypassing DPI with eBPF sock_ops
item.description: eBPF sock_ops, fake TLS packets, and why every platform needs a different hack
item.author: (Bora Tanrikulu)

item.title: I Got Sick of Remembering Port Numbers
item.description: So I built local.vibe — a friendly dashboard and local .vibe hostname for every local web app on your Mac. No more localhost:3000 vs localhost:5173 roulette.

item.title: The Best (Query) Plans of Mice and Men
item.description: Or rather, of elephants 🐘 and men. At $work, we use PostgreSQL a lot. So much in fact, that we tend to be paranoid frugal when adding even more stuff to it. In …

item.title: Carrot disclosure: Forgejo
item.description: Personal blog of Julien (jvoisin) Voisin

item.title: Stop Wasting Brainpower | Yusuf Aytas
item.description: Cognitive load, not hours, kills productivity. Cut distractions, tame complexity, and free brainpower for real engineering work.
item.author: (Yusuf Aytas)

item.title: Prompts are like prayers
item.description: I have been feeling a bit off about the recent developments in the ai/tech sector. That I wasn't able to pin point the reason for my unease, despite trying to write about it, irked me even more.

item.title: You should try contra dancing
item.description: a story of middle school Ben • a not-very-illuminating description of the mechanics • flow, joy, and community • the antidote to the rest of life • how to try contra

item.title: Using native Rails rate-limits in production | AMZ Cart Share
item.description: Learn how to use the native rate-limiting built into Rails controllers to protect your endpoints from a barrage of requests.

item.title: Why Multi Window Multi Burn Rate SLO Alerting Sucks!
item.description: Service Level Objectives (SLOs) are great! Alerts based on SLOs are great! Alerts based on SLOs promise to reduce noise by only alerting on true service degradations, reducing noise and alert fatigue. Everyone uses the multi-window multi-burn-rate (MWMB) method for defining SLO alerts, because Google recommends it. But now that I've seen how this alert behaves in the real world, I think it's an overcomplicated mess with too many moving parts that interact in confusing ways to create alert spam that is hard to explain. Simply removing some of these moving parts can improve your on-call experience.

item.title: The Moat or the Commons — Warman Notes
item.description: American capital financed AI on the assumption it would be the next great monopoly. Open-weight models are commoditizing the capability that monopoly was supposed to protect. The collision between the two now defines the direction of the U.S. AI industry — and the country.

item.title: Linux Ubuntu freeze Firefox | Vittorio Gioda
item.description: How I fixed Ubuntu freezes caused by Firefox consuming all RAM by configuring swap, swappiness, earlyoom, and zswap.
item.author: (Vittorio Gioda)

item.title: I quit drinking for a year
item.description: out of spite

item.title: How I Won a Championship That Doesn’t Exist
item.description: Poisoning the LLM knowledge supply chain with a fake Wikipedia edit and a single domain registration

item.title: How to Keep Your Brain Sharp: A Practical Playbook Beyond the Basics - The Blog of Author Tim Ferriss
item.description: The following is a guest post from Dr. Tommy Wood (@drtommywood), associate professor of pediatrics and neuroscience at the University of Washington, where his research focuses on brain health. In this blog post, Tommy covers: Tommy’s new book is The Stimulated Mind: Future-Proof Your Brain from Dementia and Stay Sharp at Any Age. Enter Tommy… […]

item.title: Offline Agentic Coding
item.description: Offline Agentic Coding: Ollama and Claude code

item.title: How I Broke the Anti-Bot Behind Nike, Kick, and Twitch — emro.cat
item.description: A full breakdown of reverse engineering a commercial anti-bot system, from VM disassembly to payload generation.

item.title: Text is the New Binary | Andrea Baccega
item.description: Software increasingly ships as prose meant to be interpreted by someone else's LLM. From Karpathy's wiki gist to gh skill install, even installation is now a paragraph. The same critique that pushes Karpathy beyond RAG hints at where the dust will eventually settle.
item.author: (Andrea Baccega)

item.title: The Accidental Ancestor — How a Computer for Verifying Numbers Shaped Modern Hashing
item.description: 1954, Hans Peter Luhn filed for a US patent on a Computer for Verifying Numbers. This is one of the earliest examples of using mathematical transformations to verify data integrity, a concept that became a foundation for modern hashes. Today you can find it under names Luhn Algorithm, Luhn Formula or Modulus 10 Algorithm.

item.title: Realtek’s 10GbE NIC performance revisited
item.description: The arrival of cheap 10GbE Realtek NICs changes the game; do we need to play it now?

item.title: Why We Measure Tickets, Not Problems Prevented — Vivian Voss
item.description: Productivity is what one can count. The decision not to deploy on Friday afternoon does not log. The engineer who prevents three outages closes no tickets that quarter. The one who ships forty-seven is promoted. The dashboard goes up. The system goes down. The chair, somewhere, is not being built.

item.title: How I Forgot How to Code — Reggie Escobar
item.description: I went all in on AI coding tools and became incredibly productive. Then I lost my job and could not write a for loop.

item.title: Agent Memory Patterns
item.description: A short HOW TO guide for agent memory systems. Especially the difference between blocks, files and skills.
